anchors.title: C2/C6 in the output (the brick)
anchors.snippet: C2/C6 in the output (the brick). The receipt must carry the **measured** incumbent (LLM-judge) run-to-run variance on the *same* test, next to the verdict — the held-out sweep where the gate is decidable and the judge flips on identical bytes. *Check:* run the demo; the output contains a measured `incumbent_variance` (judge flip-rate per drift level), NOT prose.

anchors.title: Input spec, bulletproof
anchors.snippet: Input spec, bulletproof. Inputs = a declared **Reef** (intent/vocabulary) + a **Payload** (the work), each named, timestamped, hash-verified. Lead with the **synthetic spec+reef** case (no Git-infra knowledge needed); the commit-bearer attestation is the optional second act. *Check:* documented input format; ambiguous input is rejected, not guessed.

anchors.title: One signed receipt, three readable layers
anchors.snippet: One signed receipt, three readable layers. A single ed25519 receipt: (a) verdict — coherence/drift with σ + cell placement; (b) measured incumbent variance (R1); (c) one-number **advisory** loss-ratio, flagged pre-calibration. *Check:* the receipt JSON has all three layers in one artifact, never split.

anchors.title: Recompute is the ballgame
anchors.snippet: Recompute is the ballgame. `npx thetacog-mcp prove-rice --check` on a *different* machine reproduces the receipt **byte-for-byte, exit 0**. *Check:* a stranger machine returns exit 0 + identical σ.

anchors.title: Cold-run < 5 min
anchors.snippet: Cold-run < 5 min. One pre-baked synthetic reef+payload case runs cold for someone who has never seen the architecture, in under 5 minutes. "Bring your own payload" is the follow-on, not the first run. *Check:* time a clean-room run.

anchors.title: Accept a spec as the reef
anchors.snippet: Accept a spec as the reef. The package can take an arbitrary spec document in as the reef/intent (depends on Laboratory's `reef-from-spec`; coordinate). *Check:* feed this very spec file → a reef is built from its vocabulary.

anchors.title: Honest fences baked in
anchors.snippet: Honest fences baked in. Output states: price = advisory/pre-calibration; Layer-A only ("your own machine, your own cache hierarchy, sealed locally" — NEVER "reads hardware perf counters"; `counter.rs` self-reports not-wired, test-guarded); paraphrase-invariance 0.30 named; no LLM in the trust path. *Check:* none of the banned claims appear; all four fences present.
